## FHIR Query Strategy

Since FHIR does not natively support exclusion logic, this requires **5 coordinated queries**. Application logic should:
1. **Intersect** results from Queries 1 & 3 (hypothyroidism + thyroid replacement therapy)
2. **Subtract** patient IDs found in Queries 2, 4, & 5 (excluded etiologies)

---

### ✅ QUERY 1 — Active Hypothyroidism Diagnoses (Include)
*(Primary/autoimmune/idiopathic — excludes iatrogenic subtypes)*

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E06.3,http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://snomed.info/sct|83664006,http://snomed.info/sct|21983002&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### ❌ QUERY 2 — Excluded Hypothyroidism Etiologies (Post-surgical, RAI, Drug-induced)

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E89.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.2,http://snomed.info/sct|237512005,http://snomed.info/sct|190268003,http://snomed.info/sct|5984002&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### ✅ QUERY 3 — Active Thyroid Hormone Replacement Therapy
*(Levothyroxine · Liothyronine · Liotrix · Desiccated Thyroid)*

```

```

This requires a **multi-step approach**: find patients with qualifying hypothyroidism diagnoses, on thyroid hormone replacement, then subtract those with excluded causes (post-thyroidectomy, post-RAI, medication-induced).

### 🔵 Step 1 — Combined Patient Lookup (Hypothyroidism + Active Thyroid Hormone Rx)

```

```

### 🔴 Step 3 — Exclusion: Post-Thyroidectomy & Post-Radioactive Iodine (ICD-10: E89.0)

```

### 🔴 Step 4 — Exclusion: Medication-Induced Hypothyroidism (ICD-10: E03.2)

### 💊 Step 5 — Active Thyroid Hormone Replacement Medications

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9071&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

### 📋 Code Reference

| Code | System | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| E03.9, E03.8, E03.3–E03.5, E03.0–E03.1, E02 | ICD-10-CM | ✅ **Include:** Primary/idiopathic/congenital hypothyroidism |
| `40930008` | SNOMED CT | ✅ **Include:** Hypothyroidism (general) |
| **E89.0** | ICD-10-CM | 🚫 **Exclude:** Post-procedural (thyroidectomy + RAI) |
| **E03.2** | ICD-10-CM | 🚫 **Exclude:** Medication-induced hypothyroidism |
| `722971000` | SNOMED CT | 🚫 **Exclude:** Hypothyroidism due to thyroidectomy |
| `722970004` | SNOMED CT | 🚫 **Exclude:** Hypothyroidism following radioactive iodine |

| RxNorm `10582` | RxNorm | Levothyroxine (synthetic T4) |
| RxNorm `10600` | RxNorm | Liothyronine (synthetic T3) |
| RxNorm `9071` | RxNorm | Thyroid desiccated (Armour Thyroid) |

> **Implementation note:** Subtract patient IDs from Steps 3 & 4 from the results in Steps 1 & 2. FHIR does not natively support `NOT` exclusion in a single query; post-processing of the result sets is required.

## FHIR Queries: Hypothyroidism + Thyroid Hormone Replacement (Excluding Iatrogenic Causes)

---

### 🔍 Step 1 — Hypothyroidism Conditions (ICD-10-CM)
> Explicitly lists only non-iatrogenic codes — **E03.2** (medication-induced) and **E89.0** (postprocedural: post-thyroidectomy / post-RAI) are excluded by omission.

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9&clinical-status=active&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### 🔍 Step 2 — Hypothyroidism Conditions (SNOMED CT)
> Uses `code:not` to explicitly exclude post-thyroidectomy, radioiodine-induced, and drug-induced hypothyroidism.

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://snomed.info/sct|83986005,http://snomed.info/sct|36971009,http://snomed.info/sct|190268003&code:not=http://snomed.info/sct|34234003,http://snomed.info/sct|36136002,http://snomed.info/sct|722869001,http://snomed.info/sct|735544001&clinical-status=active&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### 🔍 Step 3 — Active Thyroid Hormone Replacement (MedicationRequest)
> Covers levothyroxine (T4), liothyronine (T3), and desiccated thyroid (USP).

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10594&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

> Captures self-reported or externally documented medication use.

```

```

### 🔍 Step 5 — Combined Patient Query (`_has` intersection)
> Returns **Patient** resources satisfying **both** criteria simultaneously (non-iatrogenic hypothyroidism **AND** active thyroid hormone therapy).

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9&_has:MedicationRequest:patient:code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10594&_has:MedicationRequest:patient:status=active&_revinclude=Condition:patient&_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient

2-broad.raw_response:
All validations passed:
- ✅ **377 patients** satisfy both inclusion criteria (hypothyroid condition + thyroid hormone replacement medication)
- ✅ **0 records** for the exclusion conditions (not present in this server's data — but the codes are correct for any EHR)

Here is the complete picture of my reasoning:

### Cohort Decomposition
| Role | Resource | Code System | Details |
|------|----------|------------|---------|
| **INCLUSION** | `Patient` (_has AND) | SNOMED CT + ICD-9-CM | Primary/autoimmune/acquired/idiopathic hypothyroidism — **excluding** surgical/RAI/drug subtypes |
| **INCLUSION (AND)** | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm | Levothyroxine (all products/brands/routes), liothyronine (T3), desiccated thyroid (Armour), T3/T4 combo (Thyrolar) |
| **EXCLUSION** | `Condition` | SNOMED CT + ICD-9-CM | Post-thyroidectomy (27059002/244.0), post-RAI (40539002, 190277005, 14779006, 1137378008/244.1), postablative (237527007), drug-induced (367631000119105, 369091000119106, etc./244.3) |

### Key Discovery Notes
- Server: FHIR R4, SNOMED CT primary + ICD-9-CM secondary (no ICD-10); RxNorm product-level codes for medications
- All 613 MedicationRequests are thyroid hormone products (levothyroxine generics + Synthroid/Levotabs brands)
- VSAC `PIM_Thyroid_Replacement_Medications` (OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1078.785) used for comprehensive medication coverage
- VSAC `Hypothyroidism` (OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1222.744) used for condition codes

1-naive.raw_response:
# FHIR Queries: Hypothyroidism Population Identification

Three-arm phenotyping strategy across **7 queries**. Union all unique `Patient` IDs from results for your final cohort.

---

## 🩺 ARM 1 — Diagnosis-Based (Problem List / Encounter Diagnoses)

### 1a. ICD-10-CM Codes
Covers acquired, autoimmune (Hashimoto's), congenital, medication-induced, postinfectious, subclinical, and post-procedural hypothyroidism:

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E06.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E89.0&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

> | Code | Description |
> |------|-------------|
> | E03.0 | Congenital hypothyroidism with diffuse goiter |
> | E03.1 | Congenital hypothyroidism without goiter |
> | E03.2 | Hypothyroidism due to medicaments/exogenous substances |
> | E03.3 | Postinfectious hypothyroidism |
> | E03.4 | Atrophy of thyroid (acquired) |
> | E03.5 | Myxedema coma |
> | E03.8 | Other specified hypothyroidism |
> | E03.9 | Hypothyroidism, unspecified *(most common code)* |
> | E02 | Subclinical iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism |
> | E06.3 | Autoimmune thyroiditis — Hashimoto's |
> | E89.0 | Postprocedural hypothyroidism (post-thyroidectomy/RAI) |

---

### 1b. SNOMED CT Codes
Catches records coded with clinical terminology rather than billing codes:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://snomed.info/sct|83664006,http://snomed.info/sct|34263000,http://snomed.info/sct|237495005,http://snomed.info/sct|190268003,http://snomed.info/sct|44132006&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

> | Code | Description |
> |------|-------------|
> | 40930008 | Hypothyroidism (disorder) |
> | 83664006 | Acquired hypothyroidism (disorder) |
> | 34263000 | Hashimoto's thyroiditis (disorder) |
> | 237495005 | Subclinical hypothyroidism (disorder) |
> | 190268003 | Autoimmune hypothyroidism (disorder) |
> | 44132006 | Absence of thyroid gland (post-thyroidectomy) |

---

## 💊 ARM 2 — Medication-Based (Thyroid Replacement Therapy)

Catches patients on chronic levothyroxine (including those started externally with no formal local diagnosis), T3, or combination therapy.

### 2a. Active Prescriptions (MedicationRequest)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10641&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

### 2b. Documented Medication Use (MedicationStatement)
Captures self-reported or historically documented thyroid medications not in active prescriptions:

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10641&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject&_include=MedicationStatement:medication
```

> | RxNorm | Drug | Notes |
> |--------|------|-------|
> | 10582 | Levothyroxine (T4) | Synthroid, Levoxyl, Tirosint, Unithroid — ingredient-level code catches all brands/doses |
> | 10600 | Liothyronine (T3) | Cytomel — pure T3, less common |
> | 41493 | Liotrix | Combined T4/T3 (Thyrolar) |
> | 10641 | Desiccated thyroid | Armour Thyroid, NP Thyroid, Nature-Throid |

> ⚠️ **Brand-coded records:** Some EHRs store branded RxNorm codes (e.g., Synthroid = `203501`). If your server does not perform ingredient-level rollup, supplement with:
> ```
> MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|203501,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|892488,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|892494&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
> ```
> *(Synthroid 25mcg, 50mcg, 100mcg — extend as needed)*

---

## 🔬 ARM 3 — Lab-Based (Elevated TSH > 5 mIU/L)

Catches biochemically-flagged but not-yet-treated patients. Two queries handle the two common unit representations (numerically equivalent; separate queries required since FHIR unit matching is exact).

### 3a. TSH results reported in **mIU/L** (most common)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final,amended,corrected&date=ge2025-05-22&_sort=-date&_include=Observation:subject
```

### 3b. TSH results reported in **μIU/mL** (equivalent, alternate lab reporting)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|u[IU]/mL&status=final,amended,corrected&date=ge2025-05-22&_sort=-date&_include=Observation:subject
```

> | LOINC | Description |
> |-------|-------------|
> | 3016-3 | Thyrotropin \[Units/volume\] in Serum or Plasma — standard assay |
> | 11580-8 | Thyrotropin \[Units/volume\] in Serum or Plasma — ultrasensitive assay (detection limit ≤0.005) |

> 📅 **Date filter (`date=ge2025-05-22`):** Set to a 12-month lookback from today (2026-05-22) to surface active clinical concerns rather than historical transient elevations. Adjust to `ge2024-05-22` for 24 months, or remove entirely for all-time.
>
> 🔢 **Threshold rationale:** `gt5` reflects the common clinical threshold for overt hypothyroidism (normal upper limit ~4.5–5.0 mIU/L depending on lab). To capture borderline subclinical cases (TSH 4.0–5.0), change to `gt4`.

---

## 🔗 Cohort Assembly — Deduplication Logic

```
PATIENT COHORT = UNION(
  Arm 1a Condition.subject IDs,   // ICD-10 diagnoses
  Arm 1b Condition.subject IDs,   // SNOMED diagnoses
  Arm 2a MedicationRequest.subject IDs,  // active Rx
  Arm 2b MedicationStatement.subject IDs, // documented use
  Arm 3a Observation.subject IDs,  // elevated TSH (mIU/L)
  Arm 3b Observation.subject IDs   // elevated TSH (μIU/mL)
)
```

**Suggested sub-cohort tagging** for your QI initiative:

| Sub-group | Arms | Action |
|-----------|------|--------|
| Diagnosed + treated | 1 ∩ 2 | Routine monitoring audit |
| Treated without local diagnosis | 2 only (1 absent) | Outreach for formal problem list documentation |
| Elevated TSH, untreated | 3 only (1 & 2 absent) | Flag for clinical review / treatment initiation |
| Diagnosed but no active Rx | 1 only (2 absent) | Reconcile — may be on supplements or lost to follow-up |
1-naive.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete hypothyroidism patient population for a quality improvement initiative. From experience, I know these patients show up in our records in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code on their problem list, some are on chronic levothyroxine without the diagnosis being formally documented (often started by an outside endocrinologist), and some have biochemical evidence (elevated TSH) without yet being treated.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any hypothyroidism diagnosis (acquired, autoimmune, Hashimoto's, etc.)
2. Patients prescribed levothyroxine or other thyroid replacement therapy
3. Patients with elevated TSH lab values above 5 mIU/L

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

1-broad.raw_response:
# FHIR Queries: Hypothyroidism Population Identification

Below are **6 targeted queries** across three clinical detection strategies. Collect all `Patient` resources (via `_include`) from each result set and de-duplicate by patient ID to build your final cohort.

---

## 🔷 Approach 1 — Formal Diagnosis Codes (Condition)

Two queries required because SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM are separate coding systems that may be used inconsistently across records.

### 1a. SNOMED CT — Hypothyroidism Phenotypes

| Code | Display |
|------|---------|
| `40930008` | Hypothyroidism (disorder) — umbrella concept |
| `21983002` | Hashimoto's thyroiditis |
| `34234003` | Primary hypothyroidism |
| `83664006` | Autoimmune thyroid disease |

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://snomed.info/sct|21983002,http://snomed.info/sct|34234003,http://snomed.info/sct|83664006&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 1b. ICD-10-CM — Full Hypothyroidism Code Range

| Code | Display |
|------|---------|
| `E02` | Subclinical iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism |
| `E03.0` | Congenital hypothyroidism with diffuse goiter |
| `E03.1` | Congenital hypothyroidism without goiter |
| `E03.2` | Hypothyroidism due to medications/exogenous substances |
| `E03.3` | Postinfectious hypothyroidism |
| `E03.4` | Acquired thyroid atrophy |
| `E03.5` | Myxedema coma |
| `E03.8` | Other specified hypothyroidism |
| `E03.9` | Hypothyroidism, unspecified |
| `E06.3` | Autoimmune thyroiditis (Hashimoto's) |
| `E89.0` | Postprocedural hypothyroidism (surgical/RAI) |

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E06.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E89.0&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

## 🔷 Approach 2 — Thyroid Replacement Therapy

Two queries because medications from **outside providers** are commonly recorded as `MedicationStatement` (patient-reported/reconciled) rather than `MedicationRequest` (orders from your system).

### 2a. MedicationRequest — Active Orders in Your System

| RxNorm | Drug | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `10582` | Levothyroxine (T4) | Synthroid, Levoxyl, Tirosint |
| `10583` | Liothyronine (T3) | Cytomel |

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10583&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

---

### 2b. MedicationStatement — External/Reconciled Medications

> ⚠️ **Critical for your use case**: This captures patients on chronic levothyroxine started by an outside endocrinologist and documented during med reconciliation — the exact gap you described.

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10583&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
```

---

## 🔷 Approach 3 — Biochemical Evidence (Elevated TSH)

### 3. Serum TSH > 5 mIU/L

| LOINC | Display |
|-------|---------|
| `3016-3` | Thyrotropin \[Units/vol\] in Serum or Plasma |
| `11580-8` | Thyrotropin \[Units/vol\] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay |

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

## ⚙️ Post-Query Processing Notes

### De-duplication Logic
```
patients = union(
  conditions_snomed.subjects    ∪
  conditions_icd10.subjects     ∪
  med_requests.subjects         ∪
  med_statements.subjects       ∪
  elevated_tsh.subjects
)
```

### Recommended Refinements

| Consideration | Suggested Action |
|--------------|-----------------|
| **Old TSH results** | Add `&date=ge2024-05-22` to Approach 3 to limit to recent labs |
| **Pagination** | Add `&_count=200` and follow `Bundle.link[next]` for large populations |
| **Normal TSH on levothyroxine** | Patients in Approach 2 with *normal* TSH are your best-managed cohort — useful for QI stratification |
| **Subclinical hypothyroidism** | TSH 5–10 mIU/L with normal free T4 is a distinct subgroup; consider a separate Observation query with `value-quantity=gt5|...|m[IU]/L&value-quantity=le10|...|m[IU]/L` |
| **Desiccated thyroid (Armour/NP Thyroid)** | Consider adding RxNorm `1727493` to Approaches 2a/2b |
| **Missing diagnoses gap** | Patients found *only* in Approach 2 or 3 are your reconciliation gap — the primary QI opportunity |

1-expert.raw_response:
# FHIR Queries: Complete Hypothyroidism Population Identification

Seven queries across three clinical approaches. Collect all `Patient` resources returned via `_include` and deduplicate by `Patient.id` to form your union cohort.

---

## 🩺 APPROACH 1 — Formal Diagnosis Codes

### 1A · ICD-10-CM Problem List / Encounter Diagnoses
Covers the full E03 block (acquired hypothyroidism subtypes), E02 (subclinical iodine-deficiency), E06.3 (Hashimoto's/autoimmune thyroiditis), E89.0 (post-procedural: post-surgical or post-ablation), and E00.x (congenital iodine-deficiency syndromes).

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E06.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E89.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00.9&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

> **Key codes explained:**
> | Code | Description |
> |------|-------------|
> | E03.9 | Hypothyroidism, unspecified (most commonly used) |
> | E03.8 | Other specified hypothyroidism |
> | E03.2 | Medication-induced hypothyroidism (amiodarone, lithium, checkpoint inhibitors) |
> | E03.4 | Atrophy of thyroid, acquired |
> | E03.5 | Myxedema coma |
> | E06.3 | Autoimmune thyroiditis / Hashimoto's thyroiditis |
> | E89.0 | Post-procedural hypothyroidism (post-thyroidectomy, post-RAI) |
> | E02 | Subclinical iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism |

---

### 1B · SNOMED CT via Subsumption (`:below` operator)
The `:below` modifier traverses the SNOMED CT hierarchy under concept `40930008` (Hypothyroidism, disorder), automatically capturing all subtypes: autoimmune, Hashimoto's, primary, secondary, congenital, and subclinical — without needing to enumerate each child concept.

```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

> ⚠️ **Prerequisite:** Your FHIR server must support SNOMED CT hierarchical subsumption (common in HAPI FHIR, Azure Health Data Services, and Firely Server). If unsupported, the query silently returns only exact matches; validate with your server's `/metadata` CapabilityStatement.

---

## 💊 APPROACH 2 — Thyroid Replacement Therapy (Medication Proxy)

### 2A · Active MedicationRequests (Prescriber-Generated Orders)
Targets patients with active prescriptions for thyroid hormone replacement, capturing patients treated by outside endocrinologists whose diagnosis was never formally added to your problem list.

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|18401,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|40544&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

---

### 2B · Active MedicationStatements (Chronic Med List / Patient-Reported)
Captures medications documented during medication reconciliation that may not have generated a MedicationRequest in your system (e.g., filled at external pharmacy, reported at intake).

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|18401,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|40544&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
```

> **RxNorm ingredient codes used:**
> | RxNorm | Ingredient | Notes |
> |--------|-----------|-------|
> | `10582` | Levothyroxine (T4) | Synthroid, Levoxyl, Tirosint — by far most common |
> | `18401` | Liothyronine (T3) | Cytomel — less common, used adjunctively |
> | `40544` | Liotrix (T4+T3 combo) | Thyrolar — rarely prescribed |
>
> ⚠️ **Desiccated thyroid (Armour Thyroid, NP Thyroid, Nature-Throid):** These products have variable RxNorm encoding across EHR systems. Supplement with a text/name search or verify the local formulary code:
> ```
> MedicationRequest?medication.code:text=armour+thyroid&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
> MedicationRequest?medication.code:text=desiccated+thyroid&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
> ```

---

## 🧪 APPROACH 3 — Biochemical Evidence (Elevated TSH)

### 3A · TSH > 5 mIU/L — Standard UCUM Unit Expression
Looks back 2 years (from current date 2026-06-17). Covers both standard third-generation TSH and ultra-sensitive TSH assay LOINC codes.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final,amended,corrected&date=ge2024-06-17&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### 3B · TSH > 5 µIU/mL — Alternate UCUM Unit Expression (Numerically Equivalent)
Many lab interfaces report TSH in µIU/mL instead of mIU/L; these are numerically identical (1 mIU/L = 1 µIU/mL) but stored under different UCUM codes, so FHIR unit-aware comparisons require both queries.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|[uIU]/mL&status=final,amended,corrected&date=ge2024-06-17&_include=Observation:subject
```

> **LOINC codes used:**
> | LOINC | Description |
> |-------|-------------|
> | `3016-3` | Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma *(primary)* |
> | `11580-8` | Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma by detection limit ≤0.005 mIU/L *(ultra-sensitive)* |
>
> The 5 mIU/L threshold is above most lab upper reference limits (0.4–4.5 mIU/L), flagging both overt hypothyroidism (TSH typically >10) and high-normal/subclinical (TSH 5–10). Adjust with `gt10` if you want only overt hypothyroidism.

---

## 🔗 Union & Deduplication Strategy

```
Query Results          Extract Patient References
─────────────         ──────────────────────────────
1A (Condition ICD)  ──► Condition.subject.reference
1B (Condition SNOMED)►  Condition.subject.reference   ──► UNION
2A (MedRequest)     ──► MedicationRequest.subject.ref   (deduplicate
2B (MedStatement)   ──► MedicationStatement.subject.ref  by Patient.id)
3A (Obs mIU/L)      ──► Observation.subject.reference
3B (Obs µIU/mL)     ──► Observation.subject.reference
```

**Recommended implementation approach:**
1. Execute all 7 queries (with pagination via `_count` + `_getpagesoffset` or bundle `next` links for large populations)
2. Collect all `Patient` IDs from `_include`'d Patient resources and `subject.reference` fields
3. Build a deduplicated set: `UNION(1A, 1B, 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B)`
4. Optionally stratify into subgroups:
   - **Diagnosed + treated** = in 1A/1B AND in 2A/2B
   - **Treated without formal diagnosis** = in 2A/2B but NOT in 1A/1B → candidates for care gap closure
   - **Biochemical only** = in 3A/3B but NOT in 2A/2B → untreated elevated TSH, highest priority for clinical follow-up

---

## ⚠️ Important Caveats

| Issue | Mitigation |
|-------|-----------|
| `clinical-status=active` on Conditions may miss patients with inactive/resolved documentation styles | Run a second pass without `clinical-status` filter if population seems smaller than expected |
| SNOMED `:below` support varies | Validate against your server's CapabilityStatement for `subsumes` support |
| TSH date window (2 years) | Extend with `date=ge2022-01-01` or remove entirely to catch undiagnosed long-term patients |
| Unit mismatch for TSH | If neither 3A nor 3B returns expected results, try without units: `value-quantity=gt5` |
| Encounter-based diagnoses (E codes without problem list entry) | Supplement with `Condition?category=encounter-diagnosis` variant of query 1A |

All three queries are fully validated. Here is my complete analysis and the final FHIR queries.

---

## 🔬 Discovery & Methodology Summary

### Server Profile (FHIR 4.0.1 — Azure)
| Dimension | Finding |
|---|---|
| **FHIR version** | R4 (4.0.1) |
| **IGs loaded** | None (no US Core) |
| **Condition coding** | SNOMED CT (primary) + ICD-9-CM (legacy dual-coding) |
| **MedicationRequest coding** | RxNorm **product-level** codes (clinical drug form, NOT ingredient) |
| **Observation coding** | LOINC only |

---

### Arm 1 — Hypothyroidism Diagnoses (`Condition`)
- **VSAC source:** HL7 Patient Care WG value set `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1222.743/744/745` — cross-walked all 3 coding-system sets (SNOMED: 129 codes, ICD-10-CM: 16 codes)
- **Server reality:** Conditions carry SNOMED + ICD-9-CM; ICD-10-CM and additional SNOMED subtypes were included for future-proofing
- **Subtypes covered:** Primary, acquired, Hashimoto's/autoimmune, postoperative, post-radioiodine, postablative, myxedema, central/secondary, subclinical, transient, postpartum, drug-induced, iodine-induced, congenital
- **Validated count: 377 Condition resources**

### Arm 2 — Thyroid Hormone Replacement Therapy (`MedicationRequest`)
- **Critical insight:** Ingredient-level RxNorm code `10582` (levothyroxine) returns **0** — this Azure server requires product-level codes
- **VSAC source:** ONS "Levothyroxine Sodium" value set provided 32 generic product codes; server also carries 2 branded codes (966247 = Synthroid, 966189 = Levotabs) not in VSAC — included both
- **Formulation coverage:** Oral tablets (all 11 doses: 0.013–0.3mg), oral capsules (all 10 doses), IV injections (0.1, 0.2, 0.5mg), levothyroxine/liothyronine combination products (5 doses, covering desiccated-thyroid-equivalent regimens)

### 💡 Important Implementation Notes

1. **Union logic**: Take the union of the **patients** (not resources) referenced across all three queries — a single patient likely appears in multiple arms.

2. **Medication coverage**: Arm 2 covers all dose forms — oral tablets (generic + Synthroid/Levotabs brands), oral capsules (Tirosint), IV injection, and levothyroxine/liothyronine combination tablets (Thyrolar). The server confirmed **no standalone liothyronine or desiccated thyroid** products are present in this dataset.

3. **TSH threshold**: The `gt5` filter (> 5 mIU/L) is the widely accepted upper limit of the normal TSH range. The LOINC panel includes the standard assay (3016-3) plus two high-sensitivity TSH variants (11579-0 ≤0.05 mIU/L sensitivity; 11580-8 ≤0.005 mIU/L sensitivity) to ensure no results are missed.

4. **Condition codes span**:18 SNOMED codes cover all major subtypes (Hashimoto's, autoimmune, postoperative/postablative, radiation-induced, central/hypothalamic, subclinical, myxedema/myxedema coma, congenital) plus the ICD-9-CM umbrella code 244 for legacy-coded records.

**Alternative — subsumption modifier (for servers that support it):**
```

### Query Design Notes

| Approach | Query | Portability |
|---|---|---|
| **Explicit OR list** (recommended) | Comma-separated codes on `code` parameter | ✅ Works on all FHIR R4 servers |
| **Subsumption (`:below`)** | Single parent code with `:below` modifier | ⚠️ Requires server-side SNOMED hierarchy support |

**Why comma-separated OR is preferred here:**
- FHIR R4 comma-separated values on a single search parameter represent **OR logic** — any matching code satisfies the query
- Returns all five concept matches in **one round-trip**
- Does **not** rely on the server having a loaded SNOMED CT hierarchy (required for `:below`)

## FHIR Query: High TSH Lab Values

**TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone)** is measured by LOINC `3016-3`. The normal upper limit is typically **4.0 mIU/L** — values above this are considered elevated (hypothyroid pattern).

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt4|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mIU/L&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### Breakdown

| Parameter | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `code` | `http://loinc.org\|3016-3` | TSH (Thyrotropin) in Serum/Plasma |
| `value-quantity` | `gt4\|http://unitsofmeasure.org\|mIU/L` | Values > 4.0 mIU/L (above normal range) |
| `_include` | `Observation:patient` | Pulls linked Patient resources |

---

### Notes
- **Normal TSH range**: ~0.4–4.0 mIU/L; values **>4.0** suggest hypothyroidism
- You may also want to add **`&status=final`** to restrict to finalized results
- An alternate LOINC code for high-sensitivity TSH is `11579-0` — add it with `_OR` syntax if needed:
  ```
  Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11579-0&value-quantity=gt4|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mIU/L&status=final&_include=Observation:patient
  ```

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Elevated TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone) Observations

```
Observation?category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category|laboratory&code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5||m[IU]/L
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Observation` | Lab result resource |
| **Category** | `laboratory` | Filters to lab results only |
| **LOINC 3016-3** | Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma | Primary/most common TSH code |
| **LOINC 11580-8** | Thyrotropin [Units/volume] — detection limit ≤ 0.05 mIU/L | Sensitive (3rd gen) TSH assay |
| **value-quantity** | `gt5` in `m[IU]/L` (UCUM) | Values > 5 mIU/L (upper limit of normal) |

---

### Clinical Context

| TSH Range | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| 0.4 – 4.0 mIU/L | Normal (euthyroid) |
| 4.0 – 10.0 mIU/L | Mild/subclinical hypothyroidism |
| **> 5.0 mIU/L** | **This query's threshold (elevated)** |
| > 10.0 mIU/L | Overt primary hypothyroidism |

> **Note:** Some systems may store TSH in `mU/L` (equivalent unit). If results are missed, consider an additional query substituting `m[IU]/L` with `mU/L`. Also consider adding `&status=final,amended` to exclude preliminary results.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find lab observations for thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH, also called thyrotropin) where the value is above the upper limit of normal (typically > 5 milli-international units per liter), which suggests primary hypothyroidism.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Elevated TSH (Hypothyroidism Laboratory Evidence)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11579-0&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `code` | `http://loinc.org\|3016-3` | **Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma** — the standard TSH panel code |
| `code` | `http://loinc.org\|11579-0` | **Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Detection limit ≤ 0.005 mIU/L** — sensitive/third-generation TSH assay, widely used in modern labs |
| `value-quantity` | `gt5\|http://unitsofmeasure.org\|m[IU]/L` | PheKB threshold: **> 5 mIU/L** is flagged as elevated; UCUM `m[IU]/L` = milli-international units per liter |
| `status` | `final` | Excludes preliminary, amended-in-progress, or entered-in-error results — ensures only clinically reported values are captured |

---

### Clinical & Phenotyping Context

```
Normal TSH range:  ~0.4 – 4.0 mIU/L
PheKB cutoff:       > 5.0 mIU/L  ← this query
Overt hypothyroid: often > 10 mIU/L
```

**Why two LOINC codes?**
- `3016-3` is the legacy/standard code present in older EHR systems
- `11579-0` is the sensitive (ultrasensitive) assay code used by most modern analyzers — omitting it would create significant ascertainment gaps

**Path D patients captured here** are those with biochemical hypothyroidism who have:
- No ICD-9/10 diagnosis code (`244.x` / `E03.x`)
- No levothyroxine/liothyronine prescription

These would be **entirely missed** by a diagnosis-only query, making this TSH observation query an essential complement to any hypothyroidism phenotype algorithm.

This captures both Path A patients (who also have a diagnosis and are on thyroid replacement therapy) and Path D tricky patients (who have abnormal TSH but were never formally diagnosed or treated) — the latter being the harder cohort that a diagnosis-only query would miss.

### Logic Summary
```
Result = Patients(Query 1) MINUS Patients(Query 2) MINUS Patients(Query 3)
```
> **Note:** FHIR does not natively support negation (`NOT IN`). Combine results client-side by patient reference/ID. Consider filtering TSH to recent results (e.g., add `&date=ge2025-05-22`) to avoid stale values.

## FHIR Queries for Untreated Biochemical Hypothyroidism

This requires **three independent query groups** whose patient ID sets are then combined with set-difference logic in your application layer.

---

### ① Candidate Set — Elevated TSH Observations

```

> Alternatively, if your server stores TSH in µIU/mL (numerically equivalent), add a second pass:

```

### ② Exclusion Set A — Hypothyroidism Conditions

```

---

### ③ Exclusion Set B — Levothyroxine MedicationRequests

**3a — Inline CodeableConcept (RxNorm ingredient, for subsumption-capable servers):**
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582&status=active,completed,on-hold&_elements=subject
```

**3b — Inline CodeableConcept (explicit oral-tablet SCDs):**

```

**3c — Reference-based Medication resource (chained parameter):**

```

### Set-Difference Logic (Application Layer)

```
Candidates = patients(Query ①)
Excluded_A = patients(Query ②)
Excluded_B = patients(Query ③a ∪ ③b ∪ ③c ∪ ③d)

Untreated_Candidates = Candidates − Excluded_A − Excluded_B
```

|---|---|

| **Pagination** | Add `&_count=200` and follow `Bundle.link[relation=next]` for large result sets |
| **Most-recent TSH** | Add `&_sort=-date&_count=1` if you only want to evaluate the latest TSH per patient |

|---|---|---|

| `MedicationRequest` (34 RxNorm codes) | **Exclusion B** — levothyroxine/T4+T3 combination treatment (generic + Synthroid/Levotabs branded) | 613 records |

**Key design decisions:**
- **TSH threshold `>4.0`**: Standard upper limit of normal (most clinical labs 0.4–4.0 mIU/L); no unit qualifier needed since `3016-3` is Units/volume in serum/plasma.
- **Condition codes**: Server uses SNOMED CT only (confirmed via sampling); the 25 codes span all clinically relevant hypothyroid subtypes from the VSAC HL7/AHA value sets.
- **Medication codes**: The VSAC set (clinical drug forms) was extended with the branded RxNorm codes (`966247` = Synthroid, `966189` = Levotabs) discovered via server sampling, boosting coverage from 347 → 613 records.
- **Set-difference logic**: FHIR has no native NOT-EXISTS, so exclusion must be done client-side by differencing the patient ID sets.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Levothyroxine (Thyroid Replacement Therapy)

### Strategy
Three complementary layers of coverage:
1. **Ingredient-level** — RxNorm `10582` (levothyroxine) catches all branded and generic products
2. **Product-level** — Specific clinical drug RxNorm codes for each oral tablet strength (0.025 mg – 0.3 mg), as many EHR systems store product-level codes
3. **MedicationStatement** — Captures ongoing therapy records alongside prescriptions

---

```
// --- Layer 1: Ingredient-level search (levothyroxine, all brands/generics) ---

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication

// --- Layer 2: Product-level search by specific oral tablet strengths ---
// Covers: 0.025mg(25mcg), 0.05mg(50mcg), 0.075mg(75mcg), 0.088mg(88mcg),

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries — Levothyroxine SCD Codes (RxNorm)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966222,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966224,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966247,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966189&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query Design Notes

| RxNorm SCD Code | Description |
|---|---|
| `966222` | Levothyroxine **0.075 MG** (generic) |
| `966224` | Levothyroxine **0.125 MG** (generic) |
| `966247` | Levothyroxine **0.05 MG** — Synthroid brand |
| `966189` | Levothyroxine **0.125 MG** — Levotabs brand |

**Why this approach:**
- **Comma-separated codes** = OR logic → single round-trip to retrieve all four dose strengths
- **`status=active`** → narrows to currently active prescriptions, consistent with "chronic/ongoing therapy"
- **`_include=MedicationRequest:patient`** → pulls matching `Patient` resources in the same Bundle, avoiding N+1 lookups
- **Ingredient code `10582` intentionally excluded** — as noted, SCD-coded prescriptions typically won't match an ingredient-level token search on most servers

**Optional additions if needed:**
```
# Also capture "completed" long-term fills (chronic therapy may show as completed cycles):
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966222,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966224,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966247,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966189&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

FHIR REST API does not support direct negation (i.e., "patients *without* a condition"). Two queries are required — the **set difference** (patients in Query 1 but **not** in Query 2) must be computed client-side.

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E01,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

| Concept | System | Code |
|---|---|---|
| Levothyroxine (ingredient) | RxNorm | `10582` |
| Hypothyroidism (general) | SNOMED CT | `40930008` |
| Hypothyroidism, unspecified | ICD-10-CM | `E03.9` |
| Congenital hypothyroidism | ICD-10-CM | `E03` |
| Iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism | ICD-10-CM | `E00–E02` |

result = patients(Query 1) MINUS patients(Query 2)
```
